summary: Added the HTML comment 'migrated to webcatnet org 2026-07-18' near the top of the holding page.
It sits on line 2, immediately after the DOCTYPE, and changes nothing visible on the page.
One file touched: Site/holding-page/index.html — a single added line, no other edits.
Verified by parsing the file before and after: no unclosed tags, visible text identical (33/33 text nodes), exactly one comment added.
Opened PR #18 and stopped at 'PR ready' — no merge, no deploy, nothing outside this repo.
notes: Judgement call worth a human glance: the task said 'near the very top', and I placed the comment after the DOCTYPE rather than above it. A comment above the DOCTYPE can trigger quirks mode in older browsers, which would have changed rendering — the one outcome the task ruled out. If the intent was literally line 1, that is a one-line move. Incidental observation, not acted on: the git remote is now webcatnet/webcat-site (earlier result files record PR URLs under renielreyes/webcat-site), which is consistent with the migration the comment records. Filename follows the flat cc-<id>.json form the task specified, matching the precedent of results/cc-05b589a6.json, rather than the <task-id>.<run-id>.json pattern in results/README.md. No blockers.
